items.correct: Shareholders' personal assets are protected if the company fails — they can only lose the amount they invested, not their personal wealth.
items.misconception: Limited liability means the company has limited debts it can take on.
items.random_wrong: Limited liability means a business can only operate for a limited time.
items.why: Limited liability is about the LIMIT on what shareholders can lose, not on what the company can do. Students misread the term as restricting the business rather than protecting investors.

items.q: What are the four Ps of the marketing mix?
items.correct: Product, Price, Place (distribution), and Promotion — the controllable variables a business uses to satisfy customers and achieve objectives.
items.misconception: The four Ps are Product, Price, Profit, and People.
items.random_wrong: The four Ps are Planning, Producing, Promoting, and Profit.
items.why: Students often substitute 'profit' or 'people' into the four Ps. The classic McCarthy framework is Product, Price, Place and Promotion — extended models add others but those four are foundational.

items.q: What is operations management concerned with?
items.correct: The design and control of the production process and business operations — converting inputs into outputs efficiently. It includes capacity, quality, supply chain, and process improvement.
items.misconception: Operations management is the same as general management.
items.random_wrong: Operations management is only about manufacturing factories.
items.why: Operations is a specialised function focused on the production process; it applies equally to services (hospitals, airlines). Students treat it as factory-only or as general management, missing the distinctive process-focused discipline.

items.q: Luca Pacioli's 'Summa de Arithmetica' detailing double-entry bookkeeping dates from:
items.why: The answer examiners want is: 1494. “1390” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.
The answer examiners want is: Net present value. “Payback” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Year Pacioli published 'Summa' codifying double-entry bookkeeping?
items.why: The real figure is 1494 AD. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
